nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Baladon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
habitational name from Balstone in Buckland Monachorum or Bulstone in Branscombe (both in Devon) or Great Boulsdon in Newent (Gloucestershire). These placenames are of uncertain origin.

possibly a habitational name from Bowsden in Lowick (Northumberland), which is recorded as Bollisdon in 1250. This placename is most probably from the personal name Boll in the genitive case with -es + denu ‘(long, narrow) valley’, later replaced by dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Hamlin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, from the Norman French and Middle English personal name Hamelin, a double diminutive of Hamo (see Hammond ).
French: variant of   Hamelin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Americanized form of Finnish Hämäläinen (see Hamalainen ).

variants.name: Hamling
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Hamlin  .

variants.name: Hammond
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Old French personal name Ha(i)mon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. It frequently developed excrescent -d, giving Hamond, Haimund, and Hawmond. Alternatively, the name could derive from the Middle English personal name Hamund (Old Norse Hámundr, composed of the elements hár ‘high’ + mund ‘protection’), which may have been used in Normandy and in 12th-century eastern England, but the former explanation is more likely. The surname was sometimes confused with Almond and Ammon .

English: in the Bradford area of Yorkshire, the name is a shortened form of Ormondroyd, formerly Hamondesrode, from a lost place in Birstall (Yorkshire), named with the Middle English (Old French) personal name Hamon (1 above) + Middle English roid, a southern Yorkshire pronunciation of Old English rod ‘clearing’.
Irish: generally an importation from England, but occasionally an adopted name for Mac Ámoinn, see McCammon .

variants.name: Hamalainen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Reino, Pekka, Aimo, Arvi, Jukka, Jussi, Olavi, Pirjo, Raija, Timo, Wiljo.
Finnish (Hämäläinen): habitational name for someone from Häme province, in central Finland. This is a well-established Finnish surname dating back to the 16th century. Compare Hamlin .

variants.name: Hamlen
meanings: Found as a baptismal name. There does not appear to be any etymological connexion between this name and Hamnet. According to some the name in Hamlet's country was pronounced Amlet, and signifies 'madman' but no etymology has been suggested for such rendering. Amlet is the same name as Amleth, mentioned in the Danske Historic of Saxo Grammaticus, and Amlethus, King of Denmark and Sweden, found in Zedler's Lexicon. The name is from the Old German amal-laut, distinguished for spotlessness.

variants.name: Hamblin
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Hamlin  .

variants.name: Hamlyn
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Hamlin  .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Hamburger  .

historicEvents.title: Kingdom of England is Formed
historicEvents.description: Athelstan, king of Wessex and Mercia, formed the Kingdom of England.

historicEvents.title: Vikings Invade England
historicEvents.description: Viking raiders from Scandinavia invaded England in 980. Years later in 991, the English government paid them 10,000 in hopes for peace. The Viking raiders, however, continued to force more money from England.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hamlin de Baladon was born in 0874, in Much Marcle, Herefordshire, England. She had at least 1 daughter with Hamlin De Balladon.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Hamlin de Baladon è nata il nel 0874 nel Much Marcle, Herefordshire, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Hamlin De Balladon.
